rulings.Case Title: ROBERT RHEA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 23, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY AJDUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendant fails to demonstrate that decedent was not exposed to defendant's asbestos by being exposed to Hamilton joint compound. Sophisticated user doctrine not applicable, Hamilton is the intermediary. Defendant presents no evidence to support the bulk supplies defense. Motion moot as to false representation on punitive damages claims. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RMHNS,INC. VS. SIM & YOON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 23, 2009, line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT SIM & YOON CORPORATION Motion To Amend Cross-Complaint. Motion denied. Cross-complainant fails to comply with CCP section 1008(b) that requires an affidavit what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown. Cross-complainant fails to explain why the motion to amend is being made now, instead of in January 2009 when the motion to enforce settlement was denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BONITA WILLIAMS VS. SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 23, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. The third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is based upon personnel management activity that is insufficient to support the claim even if improper motivation is alleged. Managing personnel is not outrageous conduct beyond the bonds of human deceny. (See Janken vs. G.M. Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App. 4th 55, 80, citing Cole vs. Fair Oaks Fire Protection District (1987) 43 Cal.3d 148, 155.) No reasonable probability that defect could be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION , AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. HOODIN HAMIDI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 23, 2009, line 17, DEFENDANT HOODIN HAMIDI Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Motion to quash granted, no opposition filed. Three day notice does not comply with CCP section 1161 which requires specification of amount due. Here, September month does not show a closing date, and the three day notice is dated September 18, 2009. =(302/CWW)
